vancode/interpreter/vm

        interpret
      • interpret(vm: Vm; script: Script; startChunk: Chunk; staticString: Option[string] = none(string); globaldata = newJObject(); localData = newJObject()): string
        newVirtualMachine
      • newVirtualMachine(prefs: VMPreferences): Vm
        newVm
      • newVm(): Vm
        prewarmScriptOps
      • prewarmScriptOps(vm: Vm; s: Script)

We are implementing a stack-based Virtual Machine to execute bytecode generated by the VanCode compiler. The VM is designed to be simple and portable, easily embeddable in other Nim applications or projects that want to build DSL (domain-specific language) features on top of the VanCode framework.

Imports

chunk, value

Types

CallFrame = tuple[currentChunk: Chunk, cachedOps: CachedOps, pcIdx: int,
                  stackBottom: int, script: Script]
Operation = object
Vm {.acyclic.} = ref object
  importedModules*: Table[string, Script] ## Maps module paths to their loaded Script objects
VMPreferences = object
  enableHotCodeDetection*: bool ## Whether to enable hot code detection. When enabled, the VM will keep
                                ## track of how many times each chunk and procedure is executed,
                                ## and if they exceed the specified thresholds, they will be marked as "hot".
                                ## 
                                ## This is just a marker for now, but it can be used in the future to trigger
                                ## optimizations such as JIT compilation.
                                ## 
                                ## For JIT compilation we'll use the [libgccjit](https://github.com/openpeeps/gccjit.nim)
                                ## package to generate machine code for hot chunks and procedures at runtime
  hotProcThreshold*: int = 10 ## Threshold for marking a procedure as hot. If a procedure is
                              ## called more than this number of times, it will be marked as hot.
  hotChunkThreshold*: int = 100 ## Threshold for marking a chunk as hot. If a chunk is executed more than this
                                ## number of times, it will be marked as hot.

Consts

VMInitialPreallocatedStackSize {.intdefine.} = 64
initial size of the VM's evaluation stack. this is just an optimization to avoid frequent resizing during execution of small chunks. it can grow dynamically if needed. 
VMPreallocatedStackSize {.intdefine.} = 16
number of stack slots to preallocate when growing the stack. this is just an optimization to avoid frequent resizing during execution of small chunks. it can grow dynamically if needed.

Procs

proc interpret(vm: Vm; script: Script; startChunk: Chunk;
               staticString: Option[string] = none(string);
               globaldata = newJObject(); localData = newJObject()): string {.
    ...raises: [KeyError, ValueError, Exception], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Interpret the given chunk in the context of the provided script. 
proc newVirtualMachine(prefs: VMPreferences): Vm {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Create a new VM instance with optional preferences. If preferences are not provided, defaults will be used. 
proc newVm(): Vm {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc prewarmScriptOps(vm: Vm; s: Script) {....raises: [KeyError], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Call this once after loading from persistent cache (or after compile) to prewarm the VM's opcode cache with all chunks in the script and its imports
